_README: Copy this whole folder to levels/level-<yourname>/ then edit the values below. The _README and _doc_* keys are notes for you — the engine ignores any key it doesn't recognise, so they won't break anything, but a tidy level deletes them once you understand the shape.
_doc_startPosition: Where the player spawns, in canvas pixels (top-left of the player). Gravity then drops them onto the nearest platform below.
_doc_bounds: The world rectangle. Increase w for a longer level — the camera scrolls within it. Keep h at 540 (the canvas height).
_doc_platforms: Solid ground and ledges. Each is x,y (top-left) + width + height, in pixels. The player stands on, and is blocked by, every platform. Leave gaps between platforms to jump across.
_doc_placements: Where hazards / collectibles / power-ups sit. objectId MUST match an id in data/objects.json (e.g. gas-boiler, ice-car, oil-slick, solar-panel, wind-turbine, heat-pump). x,y is the top-left in pixels — to rest an object on a platform, set y = (platform top y) - 40.
_doc_goal: The finish-flag position (top-left, pixels). Put it at the far end of your level.
